title: JUnit 5 Readiness Knowledge Base
summary: Guidance for using EMP JUnit 5 readiness evidence during JUnit 4 to JUnit 5 modernization planning.
guide: Start with applicability. The JUnit 5 readiness pack is useful only when JUnit 4 tests or dependencies are detected.
Separate test modernization from framework modernization. JUnit 5 work often happens alongside Spring Boot, Java LTS, and build-tool upgrades, but it has its own behavior risks.
Treat static findings as triage evidence. They identify test APIs and runner assumptions before a consultant promises migration execution.

risks.name: JUnit 4 API usage
risks.whyItMatters: JUnit 4 annotations and assertions need explicit migration to Jupiter APIs or compatibility handling.
risks.recommendation: Inventory JUnit 4 imports and migrate tests in batches with validation evidence.

risks.name: JUnit 4 runners
risks.whyItMatters: RunWith-based tests often encode Spring, Mockito, parameterized, or custom runner behavior.
risks.recommendation: Review each runner and replace it with the correct Jupiter extension or test style.

risks.name: JUnit 4 dependency
risks.whyItMatters: A direct JUnit 4 dependency can keep old tests compiling while hiding incomplete migration work.
risks.recommendation: Track dependency replacement separately from source import rewrites.

risks.name: Vintage engine
risks.whyItMatters: Vintage can be useful temporarily, but it can also mask the amount of JUnit 4 still in production test suites.
risks.recommendation: Use Vintage deliberately as a transition bridge and report remaining JUnit 4 tests.

validationNotes: Catalog-backed reports prove report shape and risk vocabulary, not checkout compile success.
A passing test run is the most important validation evidence for test modernization.
Compile-only success is not enough because runner and extension behavior can fail at test execution time.
Failed validation evidence is still useful when it identifies build-tool, plugin, or runtime blockers.

benchmarks.name: JUnit 4 Samples
benchmarks.note: Small JUnit 4 examples for test API and runner migration evidence.

benchmarks.name: Mockito JUnit 4 Samples
benchmarks.note: Mockito runner and rule patterns that need extension review.

benchmarks.name: Spring Petclinic JUnit
benchmarks.note: Spring application tests with framework and JUnit migration overlap.

limits: The readiness report does not automatically migrate tests.
The readiness report does not prove test behavior is preserved.
The readiness report does not replace focused test execution after migration.
The readiness report does not infer custom runner semantics.
